title: RFA to Treasury: 45Z Tax Credit Regulation Needs â€˜Clarity, Certainty and Stabilityâ€™
content: In detailed comments submitted to the U.S. Treasury, the Renewable Fuels Association (RFA) emphasized the need for greater clarity and timely implementation of regulations for the Clean Fuel Production Tax Credit, commonly known as the 45Z credit. RFA President Geoff Cooper highlighted the creditâ€™s potential to enhance energy security, support rural economies, and promote innovation in renewable fuels and agriculture, but cautioned that delays and ambiguities are stifling investment and progress. RFA urged the Treasury to refine definitions related to qualifying sales, unrelated parties, and consolidated groups; revise emissions rounding practices; formally include climate-smart agriculture; and adjust wage and apprenticeship requirements. They also stressed the importance of swiftly implementing the Provisional Emissions Rate (PER) process and removing disincentives for alcohol-to-jet sustainable aviation fuel. Additionally, RFA advocated for the adoption of the 45ZCF-GREET model over alternatives and called for the inclusion of emissions pathways for fuels derived from corn and sorghum kernel fiber.
author: Analysts Account
